finding: ATM-GOV-0193 is marked done in the target ledger and planning mirror, but current evidence only proves the gate telemetry base CLI/schema/seal/report fixture and limited integration, not full automatic sampling across every required ATM governance node. Continuing ATM-GOV-0190 as a real M2 matched analyzer would risk treating incomplete coverage as complete data.
expectedBehavior: Before any M2 effectiveness, retirement, or matched A/B analysis, ATM should require a machine-readable registry coverage report proving each claim, gate, validator, checkpoint, close, evidence readback, git governance, runner-sync, batch, broker, team, and telemetry self-operation node is classified as instrumented, read-only-summary, out-of-scope, or not-yet-covered. Missing coverage must block causal claims and force inconclusive or a repair task, not silently pass.
evidenceOrRepro: Commands run on 2026-07-19: `node atm.mjs tasks status --task ATM-GOV-0193 --json` reports done; `node atm.mjs tasks status --task ATM-GOV-0190 --json` reports ATM_TASK_NOT_FOUND; `rg -n "gateTelemetry|gate-telemetry|telemetry seal|telemetry report|registry coverage|gate-events|telemetry" packages scripts tests release/atm-onefile release/atm-root-drop .atm/history/evidence .atm/history/tasks` shows gate telemetry source/test surfaces but no proof that all required ATM nodes emit through the shared helper or have sealed per-task summaries.
followUp: Open a governed repair/follow-up card before importing ATM-GOV-0190: add a coverage validator/report and an M2 preflight gate that refuses or marks inconclusive when required nodes are not instrumented or explicitly classified. The repair should also document that raw runtime telemetry/log/stat streams remain gitignored and only compact sealed digests may enter tracked evidence.
